item.title: I’m doing what makes me happy, even if it doesn’t make me the richest.
item.description: TlDr: I had a decent sized dream as a kid, but realized I’m happy without it. When I was a kid, I loved animals and I loved my mom who is a nurse. I wanted to be like her and work with animals by deciding to be a veterinary technician (basically a vet nurse). As I got older and actually started learning about what it all entailed, I started to realize that it would definitely be tougher and more stressful than kid me thought, but that’s just how jobs were, I figured. I should point out now that I am autistic and can stress easily, but I have acknowledged that there isn’t really a stress free path in life, so I kept going. Americans like me are usually told that you need to go to college to get a good job and money and all that junk, so I didn’t think I should settle for anything less. After a few more years I got excepted to a veterinary technician program at a college. Once I was finally learning everything I needed and getting hands on experience in a controlled environment, I started to wonder if this was really what I truly wanted, but I felt that I had come too far to back out now. Classes were tougher than what I was used to. I went from As and Bs to being a C average, but I kept going. While all this was going on, I was working part-time at a small animal shelter to gain some more experience for my resume and because I enjoyed it. I knew I would eventually have to leave for better opportunities, but I was staying for as long as I could. Eventually, the vet tech program had a class that required me to work at a clinic, not the shelter, to gain credits. Due to the fact that my only job experience in the field was that shelter, a shelter that was literally a remodeled house and wasn’t exactly well known, finding a job at a clinic before the semester with the class came up was difficult, however; a coworker of mine who also worked at a fancier shelter that had multiple departments, including an onsite clinic, was able to help me get a job there. The plan was long but simple: start as a kennel tech (one of the lowest positions), work hard to get promoted to the veterinary department, and then use that experience to get a better chance of getting a job at an actual clinic. I noticed the issue not too long after I started: being a kennel tech was so much more fun and less stressful than what I was learning in school. I was enjoying myself, and due to how unfair the pay in the veterinary field is in general, I was making only two dollars less than the actual vet techs in the building (and in a lot of places nearby, according to what Indeed says about vet tech salaries in the area). I was in denial for a while because I didn’t want all the money that would be the inevitable student loan debt to be for nothing, but after I was one of the lucky people to have their loans forgiven, I really started questioning if I really wanted to go through with moving up. I know those two dollars I’m not making would probably add up to something in the long run, and that paying bills and rent will be more difficult without it, but I realized that I am currently content and happy with where I am now and just simply don’t want to be “better” or go as far as I can in life because I don’t want to go farther than here, aside from wanting to save up enough to get the **** out of Texas. Sorry for the long block of text; the current version of the mobile app I’m using doesn’t seem to have an Return key to divide this into paragraphs, aside from when I divided the TlDr up there. Not sure why it only worked once, but oh well.
item.dc:creator: RedDragonDaker

item.title: My wife has been fighting hard to get approved for organ transplant, and we were just featured on our local news!
item.description: My wife Christy is a Psychology Professor in Rochester NY and she was on our local news this morning! :) https://13wham.com/news/local/community-rallies-to-support-womans-cystic-fibrosis-battle Christy was born with Cystic Fibrosis, and despite her entire life being overwhelmingly full of doctor visits, hospital visits, home medical treatments, etc. We have managed to build a very functional life together, and she was in good health up until September of this year. She got a cold, and it really tanked her. We thought that's all it was, just a really nasty cold. Luckily, she had a doctors appointment already scheduled for that week, when she went to that checkup, they were able to recognize multiple symptoms of liver failure that she and I had not known to look for. They rushed her to the hospital, and that night she crashed. She was intubated in the ICU for weeks. Her lungs were full of pneumonia, she was in liver failure, she was in kidney failure, her blood was septic from an unknown source they never found, her blood also had so much ammonia in it that they were sure if she survived she would have brain damage. They told me more than once that she likely wouldn't survive, and to be ready to say goodbye. Well, she pulled through and eventually woke up. For weeks she couldn't talk, eat solid foods, or even braid her own hair, let alone stand up and walk to the bathroom. She fought and worked her way up to a point that they were comfortable sending her home, though still requiring dialysis 3 times a week, and a LOT of professional-nursing and husband-nursing at home. Christy has been home for 5 weeks, and while she's still very sick, and more frail than I like, she has been working incredibly hard, and we've managed to keep her at home and out of the hospital thus far! She's in critical need of a double lung and liver transplant, and we're still working with the transplant specialists in Pittsburgh to get all the tests and procedures they require before she can be approved for transplant. It's a long, exhausting, and really frustrating process just to get her approved, but we're getting close, and we feel hopeful about her chances once she gets approved and listed. We are more fortunate than many. Our insurance should cover the bulk of the Healthcare costs for the transplant, leading up to it, and the care afterwards, but the transplant advisors have made it clear that there will be tens of thousands of dollars of costs throughout the process that are not covered by insurance, in addition to the fact that Christy is unable to work for many months, possibly for life. If we have to sell our house, no problem, we'll do that, but if we can keep the home we've built a life in, that'd be nice. I wish we had enough savings that we could pay for her medical needs ourselves. I wish we lived in a society where medical care was a priority and covered for all citizens. Unfortunately, we don't live in that society, and we need help if she's going to get the treatment opportunity we're hoping for. Below is the link to Christy's transplant fundraiser page. From the bottom of our hearts, we so dearly appreciate all of the support we've received, monetary and otherwise. Thank you everyone and Merry Christmas. https://cota.org/cotaforfesslerstrong/
item.dc:creator: Groucho713

item.title: At least I slept well…
item.description: Yesterday was it I think. She came home wasted after promising several times now not to. I don’t know how to raise our child alone and now she scares the hell out of me. Cleaned entire house yesterday only to have her ruin the entire place again. I know what I have to do but I don’t know what’s best for my son…

Apt is the best part of Debian. Makes me feel like a hacker.
:)
Excellent save
Someone should do that in a film.
The whole "Celeb wears thing twice" thing is just like...who cares. She likes this item, she wears it, she looks good. Calm down. Then again people who give a shit about the Royals are a special kind of weird.
Tag yourself I'm middle right.
Looking at you Musk.
Not many other uses for CRTs other than that.
Those chops
People will see a sign saying "don't do this, you will die" and do it anyway because fuck you. Case in point: Cave Divers.
Note to my discord friendos, I'm thinking about making this a memecomp
G Gauge vs N Gauge let's GOOOOOOOO
That is like the most insane idea I have ever heard.
"transgender genital check" just seems like a scheme for Pedophiles to weaponise Transphobia to make "Penis inspection Day" real.
Malcom X apparently left NOI for Mainstream Sunni Islam after this trip.
me_irl.
me, tis me. a.
See this? Queen shit.
Me when I worked in an office.
I always had this idea for something called "Casual LUG", a Linux User Group for people who use Linux casually rather than for Coding and shit,
oh no
:3

item.title: I have trapped a wild void

item.title: Got to tour the Paris Catacombs recently
item.description:
This was how far underground we were, in old limestone caves. The limestone was used to create many of the old buildings in Paris, like Notre Dame.
But many of the caves were left unstable and could collapse causing damage to the streets and buildings on the surface. Once they were inspected and stabilized, these numbers and letters represented how many (5) did this person (J) inspected and what year (1847).
This was at the beginning of the tour, and don’t remember what it was exactly
Definitely not allowed in here
This was sculpted from memory by a worker on his lunch breaks, who was imprisoned in Menorca who could see the port from his cell.
This is the prison in Menorca.
This is the plaque stating “This work was started in 1777 by Decure […] veteran of his Majesty and was finished in 1782.” He was killed in a cave-in.
The limestone creates water so clear, it’s hard to tell that the stairs are almost completely submerged.
You can barely see the water line it is so clear.
Long walkway leading up to the ossuaries (where the bones are stored)
This is our tour guide Charlotte who was amazing! I could only post 1 minute of the video, so she corrected her Roman numerals after it cuts off.
What she was reading.
Entrance to the ossuary
Bones!
New meaning to the phrase “Love you to death”
This was lit very orangey - no idea why
There were many bones with some kind of hole in the skull
These are not real tombs, they are to camouflage the support walls
This was in a section that was being restored.
Holes and mold
These might just be the gates to the underworld
So. Many. Bones.
This looked like a barrel.
As above, so below. Good movie. Creepy to be down here after watching it though
This was a sinkhole that goes all the way up to street level. Shortly after this is the stairway that leads out. We were also told there are illegal tours of sections that are not open to the public. You can get very lost if you are not with someone who knows where they are going.
item.dc:creator: KerowynsDragon
